# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:20.04

# Install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    nginx \
    python3-flask \
    python3-pip \
    postgresql \
    redis-server \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set up the web server
COPY ./web /var/www/html
RUN chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/html

# Set up the API server
COPY ./api /app
WORKDIR /app
RUN pip3 install -r requirements.txt

# Configure Nginx
COPY ./nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/default

# Expose ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Start services
CMD service nginx start && \
    service postgresql start && \
    service redis-server start && \
    python3 app.py

# Use a non-root user for security
RUN useradd -m appuser
USER appuser

# Final command to run the application
CMD ["bash", "-c", "service nginx start && service postgresql start && service redis-server start && python3 /app/app.py"]
